checklist.item1: YES — first three MCP tool calls: browser_navigate(url: 'http://127.0.0.1:3002') at step 1, browser_snapshot() at step 2, browser_evaluate(expression: "document.querySelector('.status-text')?.textContent?.trim()") at step 3. MCP tool call format throughout; no standalone Node.js script.
checklist.item2: YES — test-plan.md lines 12-21 reproduce all 9 Acceptance Flow steps verbatim from spec.md. Phases 1-7 implement each step in sequence.
checklist.item3: YES — user input delivered by real Playwright APIs: browser_click(element: '.xterm-helper-textarea') at step 13, browser_type(text: 'echo QUEUED_MESSAGE') at step 14, browser_press_key(key: 'Enter') at step 15. All browser_evaluate() calls read DOM state only — none drive user actions.
checklist.item4: YES — ASSERTION 3b at step 21 uses browser_evaluate to read document.querySelector('.xterm-rows')?.textContent, asserting terminal content contains 'QUEUED_MESSAGE'. Observes actual rendered terminal text, not WS connection status alone.
checklist.item5: YES — test-plan.md 'Pre-fix expected output' documents: boolean connected=signal(false) in store.js → TopBar has no Reconnecting branch → step 11 evaluate returns 'Disconnected' not 'Reconnecting...' → ASSERTION 1 fails. Causal chain fully specified.
checklist.item6: YES — all 5 Then conditions from spec.md covered: (1) Reconnecting state → steps 10-11, (2) message queued while disconnected → steps 12-16 and ASSERTION 3b, (3) reconnect restores Connected + delivers queue → steps 19-21, (4) retries exhausted → Disconnected → steps 24-25, (5) retry details in Logs tab only → steps 26-29.
